title: Config repair is impossible once the stored config is invalid (validate-before-mutate)
description: amendConfigEntryForDir loads the existing config (which validates it) before applying any mutation — only a dryRun option, no force/skip — so an already-invalid config cannot be repaired through the CLI: the write path refuses on the pre-existing invalid state before the corrective mutation runs. accept-all is not a recovery path either — it never-clobbers a populated config. Independent of the missing load-time migration (FGAP-095): a config whose schema_version equals the current schema version (no migration applies) but whose body is invalid (corruption, hand-edit, or a schema change with no registered migration) throws at BOTH loadConfigForDir and amendConfigEntryForDir — unmigratable AND unrepairable. Migrate-on-load resolves only the version-gap subset of the deadlock.

evidence.reference: amendConfigEntryForDir loads (validates) the config before mutating; only a dryRun option, no force/skip — an already-invalid config throws on load before the corrective write
accept-all (adoptConception) never-clobbers a populated config, so it is not a recovery path for an invalid config
loadConfigForDir validates the raw config on every load, so any read of an invalid config also throws — the read path and the repair path share the same pre-validation

impact: Once the stored config is invalid for any reason not covered by a registered migration, there is no sanctioned CLI path back to validity — the only recourse is the forbidden direct-edit of .context/config.json. This is the recovery-side root cause of the incident's unrecoverability: even setting aside the missing migration, validate-before-mutate means the CLI cannot dig the substrate out of an invalid state. Proven independent of FGAP-095 in the /tmp repro (a no-version-delta invalid config, both unmigratable and unrepairable).
canonical_vocabulary: recovery / repair path (validate-after-mutate, or a force-repair write that validates the result rather than the pre-existing state)
proposed_resolution: Provide a sanctioned recovery path that does not refuse on a pre-existing invalid config: a repair/force write (or a recover op) that applies the corrective mutation and validates the RESULT, rather than validating the stored config on load and throwing before the repair can be applied. Scope is distinct from the load-time migration (FGAP-095): migration carries a version-gap config forward; this restores any invalid config regardless of version. Sibling of FGAP-095 in the config-schema-lifecycle root-cause set.
